subject.description: A man in his early 50s, European, salt-and-pepper stubble, wearing a simple dark linen shirt with sleeves rolled to forearms. Skin pores visible, slight crow's feet around eyes. Relaxed posture, arms folded behind his head, leaning back in a wooden chair. Micro expression: corners of mouth barely lifted on one side, eyes half-closed with the contentment of someone who just solved a puzzle they spent months on. Chin slightly raised.
subject.action: Leaning back in his chair with arms folded behind his head, gazing at a laptop screen with quiet satisfaction.

setting.location: A warm creative home office with a wall behind him covered in colorful sticky notes and hand-drawn workflow diagrams. A single laptop open on a worn wooden desk. A half-empty coffee cup beside it. Real lived-in space with books stacked messily.

composition.camera_angle: Eye-level, slightly off-center to the right
composition.framing: Full medium shot. Top frame: crown with generous headspace. Bottom frame: hip level showing the chair and desk edge. Rule of thirds, subject positioned left third. Wall of sticky notes fills background.

lighting.type: Warm golden hour sunlight streaming through a large side window, casting long soft shadows across the desk. Natural bounce light filling the shadows gently.
lighting.color_grading: Warm amber tones with soft contrast, slightly desaturated shadows

mood: Quiet triumph. The relief of figuring something out after months of struggle.
aesthetic_style: 8K ultra realistic, hyper realistic photorealism. Shot on Canon EOS R5.
